answer.content:
HTTPS Interactive Simulator (Plain UI)

Client Configuration
Client options: change TLS version, cipher list and SNI. Many handshake paths depend on these values.
No client cert
Server Configuration
No server cert
Trust Store (Root CAs)
A root CA can be generated and used to sign server certificates. Trust store determines if a certificate is accepted.
Attacker / Network
Handshake Controls & Simulation
Speed (ms delay between steps): 400 ms
Handshake Log 

      

    


    

      Message Inspector & Tools
      
      

      

        
        
        
      

    


    

      Derived Keys / Secrets (for advanced users)
      
After handshake completes, symmetric keys derived via ECDH/HKDF will appear here and can be used to decrypt encrypted frames.

messages.content:
Generate a single, self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript that demonstrates the concept HTTPS with MAXIMUM interactivity and rich user control. Focus on:

- Multiple interactive controls (buttons, sliders, input fields)
- Complex state transitions and user-driven workflows
- Deep exploration capabilities with many possible interaction paths
- Rich functionality as the PRIMARY goal

IMPORTANT: Keep visual design EXTREMELY MINIMAL and BASIC - use simple black text on white background, basic HTML elements with no styling, no colors, no animations. The interface should be functional but visually plain and unpolished. Prioritize interactive richness over visual appeal.

Only respond with the complete HTML file.
